name: Wadada Leo Smith
abstract: Ishmael Wadada Leo Smith (born December 18, 1941) is an American trumpeter and composer, working primarily in the field of creative music. He was one of three finalists for the 2013 Pulitzer Prize for Music for Ten Freedom Summers, released on May 22, 2012.

works_created.t: Five Piano Pieces: 1a. Brilliant Spinding Sonic Velocities

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I still do things with Roscoe Mitchell and Wadada Leo Smith and Henry Threadgill, well that connection has remained over all these years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 7, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Jane Ira Bloom
about_quotes.evidence: Leo Smith... they all happened to be there at that one time... opportunities to play with like-minded players, and we created a scene there
about_quotes.doc_title: Jane Ira Bloom interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 3, 1998
